common.tagline: Your AI for code, trends, and everything in between.

welcome.title: Welcome to Spark.
welcome.subtitle: Your AI for code, trends, and everything in between.
welcome.byokBody: Paste your DeepSeek key once. It's encrypted on this device and never touches our servers.
welcome.localBody: Your chats, files, and settings live on your device. No accounts, no telemetry, no tracking.
welcome.anywhereBody: Same conversation history across web, desktop, mobile, and browser extensions (coming soon).
welcome.getKeyLink: Grab a DeepSeek key in 60 seconds

chat.emptySubtitle: Your AI for code, trends, and everything in between.
chat.promptReviewCode:
Review this code and suggest improvements:

```
// paste code here
```
chat.promptSeoTrends: What are the top SEO trends and ranking factors right now?
chat.promptFashion: What are the current fashion trends in Tokyo for this season?
chat.promptExplain: Explain async/await in JavaScript with a real-world example.
chat.disclaimer: Spark can make mistakes. Verify important info.
chat.conversationsEmpty: No conversations yet. Start a new chat to begin.

apiKey.title: DeepSeek API key
apiKey.description: Spark is bring-your-own-key. Your key is encrypted on this device and never sent to any server we control. Paste it once and you're set.
apiKey.getKeyLink: Get a free key on platform.deepseek.com
apiKey.label: API key
apiKey.baseUrlHint: Use a custom URL only if you're proxying DeepSeek behind your own gateway. Defaults to {default}.
apiKey.removeConfirm: Remove your DeepSeek API key from this device?
apiKey.isSet: API key is set on this device.

model.title: Default model
model.description: The model used for new conversations. You can switch any time from the chat header.
model.temperatureHint: Lower is more focused, higher is more creative. Default 0.7.
model.deepseekReasonerDesc: Step-by-step reasoning for code & complex problems (DeepSeek-R1)

language.title: Language
language.description: The Spark interface language. The assistant will reply in this language unless you switch mid-thread.

prompt.title: System prompt
prompt.description: Customize how Spark behaves. This text is prepended to every conversation.
prompt.placeholder: You are a helpful AI assistant…

about.subtitle: Local-first AI for code, trends, and style.
about.promise1: We do not collect, store, or transmit your prompts.
about.promise2: We do not see your API key. Ever.
about.promise3: We do not embed analytics, trackers, or third-party scripts.
about.promise4: Chat history lives only on your device. Clear it anytime.
about.promise5: Source is open. Verify the claims above in our repo.
about.dangerDescription: Wipes all chats, your encrypted API key, and any local settings. This cannot be undone.
about.dangerConfirm: Permanently delete all chat history and your stored API key on this device?
about.dangerSuccess: All local data has been removed.

privacyPage.title: Spark is local-first.
privacyPage.intro: Most AI products are built on a promise: trust us. Spark is built on a different promise: you don't have to. Your prompts, your chat history, and your API key never leave your device. Here's exactly how, in plain language.
privacyPage.promise1Body: Spark never sees what you type. The /api/chat route is a stateless proxy: it forwards your request to DeepSeek and immediately discards it. No logs, no database, no analytics.
privacyPage.promise2Body: Your DeepSeek key is encrypted on this device using WebCrypto AES-GCM. On desktop builds, the key is passed directly from the encrypted store into a Rust streaming command that calls DeepSeek over HTTPS — no server in between.
privacyPage.promise3Body: No Google Analytics. No Plausible. No PostHog. No Sentry. No fonts loaded from CDNs. Open the Network tab — the only host Spark contacts is the one running the model.
privacyPage.promise4Body: Conversations are persisted to localStorage (web/PWA), Tauri app data dir (desktop), or platform-specific storage (mobile/extensions when those phases ship). Clear it anytime from Settings → About → Erase everything on this device.
privacyPage.promise5Body: Every line of code is on GitHub under the MIT license. Read /api/chat, read lib/keystore.ts, read the Rust chat_stream command. If we ever break a promise, the diff will be there for the world to see.
privacyPage.limitsBody: We're honest about what our encryption can and can't do. On the web platform, the WebCrypto layer defends against trivial disk inspection (someone reading localStorage from a browser backup) but not against malicious scripts that run in the same origin. Real protection comes from the platform: the desktop app stores keys in an OS-restricted directory, mobile builds use the OS keychain + biometric unlock, and VS Code uses the SecretStorage API.
privacyPage.footer: This page is versioned with the rest of the source — see the commit history for any updates. Questions? Open an issue on GitHub.

error.title: Something went sideways.
error.body: Spark hit an unexpected error while rendering this view. Your chat history and settings are safe — they live on this device, untouched.
error.globalBody: A critical error prevented Spark from rendering. Your data on this device is unaffected — refreshing usually fixes it.
